hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Crop PNG images with precision while preserving transparency. Fast, free, and privacy-focused—everything happens in your browser.

howTo.title: How To Crop PNG Images Online
howTo.step1Desc: Drag your PNG file into the editor or click to browse. The image loads instantly in your browser.
howTo.step2Desc: Pick a preset ratio or go freeform. Drag the selection handles until the frame covers exactly what you want to keep.
howTo.step3Desc: Hit crop, check the preview, and download. Transparency stays intact, zero quality loss.
howTo.tipText: PNG format preserves transparency—perfect for logos, icons, and graphics with transparent backgrounds.

why.title: Why Crop PNG Images?
why.p1: PNG is the only widely supported format that keeps transparent backgrounds intact. When you crop a logo, icon, or UI element as PNG, the see-through areas survive the trim. That means you can drop the cropped file straight into a design comp or web page without worrying about white boxes showing up behind it.
why.p2: Cropping also trims wasted canvas pixels that inflate file weight for no visual payoff. A tighter frame on a PNG icon can cut its file size noticeably, which adds up fast when you are serving dozens of assets on a single page.
why.b1: Keep the alpha channel alive so logos and icons look clean on any background colour.
why.b2: Trim dead canvas space from Figma or Photoshop exports to tighten up your asset library.
why.b3: Deliver pixel-perfect icons and UI elements at exactly the dimensions your layout expects.

features.title: Key Features
features.presetRatiosDesc: 9 preset aspect ratios • Free crop • Lock option
features.privateDesc: All processing happens in your browser

proTips.title: Pro Tips for PNG Cropping
proTips.t1: Use 1:1 for app icons and favicons. Most app stores and browsers expect a perfect square, so locking the ratio saves you a trip back to the editor later.
proTips.t2: Trim your logo as tightly as possible. Extra padding around a PNG logo wastes space and makes alignment harder when you paste it into a layout.
proTips.t3: If file size matters more than transparency, crop here and then run the result through the WebP converter. You will get the same transparency at a much smaller weight.
proTips.t4: For photographs that have no transparency, switch to the JPG cropper instead. JPEG files are dramatically smaller than PNG for full-colour photos.

items.question: Does cropping a PNG keep the transparent background?
items.answer: Yes, fully. The cropper reads every pixel including the alpha channel, so transparent regions stay transparent in the output. You can drop the cropped file straight into a design tool or web page and the background will remain see-through.

items.question: Will cropping reduce the quality of my PNG?
items.answer: Not at all. PNG is a lossless format, which means no pixel data gets thrown away during encoding. The cropped image is an exact copy of the selected region, down to the last colour value.

items.question: Can I crop a JPEG here and get a PNG out?
items.answer: Yes. Upload any common image format and the tool will crop the area you select, then encode the result as a lossless PNG. Keep in mind that converting a JPEG to PNG will not recover quality that was already lost during JPEG compression.

items.question: Why are my cropped PNG files so large?
items.answer: PNG uses lossless compression, so it keeps every pixel intact. Photographs with millions of unique colours produce big PNGs. If you need a smaller file and can accept lossy compression, run the output through a WebP or JPEG converter afterward.

items.question: Is anything uploaded to a server during cropping?
items.answer: Nothing at all. The entire crop and encode pipeline runs locally inside your browser using a canvas element and a WebAssembly PNG encoder. Your image never leaves your machine.
